meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solving Slow Development
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with slow development. Learn how to master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide for AI builders.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from lack of technical scoping and unmanaged state complexity. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for rapid CLI-driven execution, you transform your workflow from reactive debugging to proactive engineering. This structured approach allows you to ship production-grade features in hours rather than weeks.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your codebase feels like a labyrinth of spaghetti logic. You started with a vision of a flagship project, but now you are drowning in technical debt, losing hours to minor bugs, and questioning if you have what it takes to ship. This is the 'Developer’s Plateau,' a rite of passage for every senior engineer, but it does not have to be your permanent state.

True velocity isn't about typing faster; it is about managing the intent of your code before you write a single character. By mastering the synergy between architectural planning and AI-driven implementation, you can bypass the friction that slows down your peers. Let's turn your development process from a bottleneck into an engine.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Before touching code, map out your data models and component relationships to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Create a high-level schema and component dependency graph for a [Insert Project Name] SaaS application. Focus on modularity and database scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --plan 'Create project directory structure based on the architectural schema provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the repetitive structure so you can focus on core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, type-safe boilerplate for a [React/Node.js] project. Include standard directory patterns, essential middleware, and environment configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate boilerplate files based on the structure defined in step 1.'

steps.title: Establish the State Management Strategy
steps.description: Centralize state to avoid the common pitfalls of prop-drilling and unpredictable data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a state management solution for a project of this scale. Compare Redux vs. Zustand and provide the implementation code for a global auth state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the chosen state management solution into the current codebase.'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Feature Loops
steps.description: Break features into atomic PRs to ensure consistent progress and easy debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the core feature [Insert Feature] into 3 distinct, testable development tasks with clear acceptance criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute task 1 of the feature breakdown: [Insert Task Name].'

steps.title: Automated Code Review and Refinement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform self-critique on your implementation before committing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for performance bottlenecks and adherence to clean code principles. Suggest specific refactors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review --fix 'Apply recommended refactors for performance and readability.'

pro_tips: Always define your 'Definition of Done' for every function before you write the implementation logic.
Treat your CLI history as a log; use Claude Code to summarize your daily progress into a README change-log.
When stuck, use Antigravity to simulate a 'Rubber Duck' session by pasting your error logs into a dedicated debugging prompt.
Keep your context window clean by clearing your Claude Code session every 5-10 commits to avoid hallucinated dependencies.
Prioritize writing integration tests over unit tests; they provide more confidence in your project's overall health as a student.
content.next_steps: After completing your first core feature, deploy your project to a staging environment and document the architecture in your portfolio to showcase your engineering maturity.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to outpace the competition. Stop coding blindly and start building with intention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
